column1: If we fail to maintain effective internal control procedures over our financial reporting and disclosures, investor confidence may be
adversely affected thereby affecting the value of our stock price.
We are required to maintain proper internal control over our financial reporting and adequate controls related to our disclosures. Under
applicable regulations, internal control over financial reporting is defined as a process designed by, or under the supervision of the Chief
Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer, to provide reasonable assurance regarding the reliability of financial reporting and the
preparation of financial statements for external purposes in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles. If we fail to
maintain adequate controls resulting in a material weakness in our internal control over financial reporting, and/or if we are unable to
remediate a material weakness on a timely basis, our business, results of operations, financial condition and/or the value of our stock
